steps.title: Recognize the repeating expression
steps.content: The expression $13 - 5x$ appears twice in the equation. Rather than expanding the parentheses on both sides, we use substitution to simplify the problem.

steps.title: Substitute u = 13 - 5x
steps.content:
Let $u = 13 - 5x$. Replacing the expression in the equation gives:
$$-7u + 3 = -8u + 21$$
Now we have a linear equation in $u$ instead of a more complex problem in $x$.

steps.title: Solve for u
steps.content:
Add $8u$ to both sides:
$$-7u + 8u + 3 = 21$$
$$u + 3 = 21$$
Subtract 3 from both sides:
$$u = 18$$
Since $u = 13 - 5x$, the value of $13 - 5x$ is 18.
